items.title: Weather warning for abnormally high tides along entire SA coastline

children.content: South Australia's State Emergency Service warns people not to risk their safety as abnormally high tides are set to lash the state's coastline.

gallery.altText: A jetty with a wave hitting it
A man wearing orange filling a sandbag with a yellow machine in front of sheds
Flooding over a road with a supermarket

thumbnail.altText: A jetty with a wave hitting it
A man wearing orange filling a sandbag with a yellow machine in front of sheds

items.title: Expensive watches, work bags and gold-plated stationary claimed in SA Health scheme

children.content: The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) has called for stronger governance of professional development reimbursements for public sector medical officers, with some staff making claims for luxury items like watches and work bags.

image.altText: A watch with a digital screen on a man's wrist with numbers on it.

items.title: Inquest considers whether care of Adelaide man's bed sores was 'appropriate and reasonable'

children.content: A coronial inquest will investigate whether an aged care facility in Adelaide's inner north appropriately treated the bed sores a 79-year-old man with dementia developed in the lead-up to his death.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: Quicksand causes terrifying ‘Indiana Jones moment’ for second Adelaide beachgoer

children.content: Days after a young Adelaide beachgoer had to be rescued from quicksand, a second person has spoken out about a similar experience along the same stretch of coast, saying she was momentarily overcome by "complete fear".

gallery.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

items.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft

children.content: Fuel theft is prompting farmers to install CCTV cameras at unprecedented rates, leading one security company to overhaul its entire business model.

image.altText: A man in hi-vis and a cap stands in front of a diesel tank.

items.title: Tough times for hospitality businesses with rising cost of food and power

children.content: The hospitality sector is grappling with "unprecedented" cost increases, according to a national peak body, with many being forced to close their doors.

image.altText: A woman leans an arm on a food counter in a restaurant

items.title: Ex-soldier, comedian and aspiring Martian is the Aussie face of Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

articles.title: Two men believed trapped in cave for nearly two weeks as rescue mission turns to vertical tunnels

children.content: Rescue efforts for two remaining men believed to be trapped in a cave in central Laos have shifted to investigating deep vertical tunnels in the jungle.

image.altText: A man in an orange helmed climbs down a narrow cave, with the only light coming from headlamps.

gallery.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.
Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

thumbnail.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.
Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

items.title: Labor MP calls for AUKUS 'renegotiation'

children.content: An outspoken backbencher points to changes that will see Australia buy three used Virginia-class submarines as a catalyst to consider changes.

image.altText: A man in a suit is questioned by reporters in a hallway

items.title: Farm profits to dive by 70 per cent as drought and costs bite

children.content: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

image.altText: Two hands pull gently on wheat in a paddock.

items.title: Paddle-steamer first launched 120 years ago returns to working life

children.content: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

image.altText: The PS Canally is moored on a river. It is white and grey with colourful flags hung up on it.

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over golf course redevelopment

children.content: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Adelaide Hills man pleads guilty to murdering mother

children.content: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

image.altText: A bald man with a beard wearing handcuffs and a plaid shirt

items.title: 'Bit of a gold rush' as companies search for underground hydrogen

children.content: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

image.altText: A drill rig at night with trucks and lights surrounding it.

items.title: Russian strike on Ukraine leaves multiple dead and others feared trapped

children.content: A major Russian attack on parts of Ukraine and its capital, Kyiv, has killed multiple people, damaged buildings and left others feared trapped under rubble, local authorities say.

image.altText: Firefighters hose down building ruins, smoke billows.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

image.altText: A woman with stands next to a wooden stall stocked with loaves of bread. She wears jeans and a tshirt, smiling

items.title: This detective was told his colleagues were attacked. Then he saw the footage

children.content: As the footballer, shirt torn, face bloodied and with a concussion, paced his police cell, a veteran detective's phone started to ring.

image.altText: A close up of a man's face, he is standing outdoors looking straight ahead with a serious, slightly sad expression.

items.title: What the polls reveal about One Nation's growing appeal

children.content: The One Nation leader, whose popularity has soared in the past 12 months, would be the first to say she doesn't put too much stock in the numbers.

items.title: One Nation's David Farley sworn in as Coalition resolves to vote against tax package — as it happened

children.content: One Nation's David Farley has been sworn in as the MP for Farrer. Meanwhile, the Coalition has resolved to vote against the government's tax package.

image.altText: David Farley signs his oath in the lower house.

items.title: Veteran broadcaster Alan Jones has one of charges against him dropped

children.content: Prosecutors drop one of the criminal charges against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones less than two months before the start of his trial, reducing the number of complainants in his indecent assault case.

image.altText: An old man in a suit walks with his male lawyer amongst a media scrum.

items.title: Woman shares incredible survival story after WA's strongest storm in 49 years

children.content: A driver says she almost died in a freak car accident on a highway in regional WA during Sunday's wild weather.

children.content: South Australia's State Emergency Service warns people not to risk their safety as abnormally high tides are set to lash the state's coastline.

items.title: Tax deduction red flags on the ATO's radar

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tax-time tips, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

image.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

items.title: Copper thieves are more brazen and taking exceptional risks, demolition experts say

children.content: Demolition sites are being stripped for copper at an escalating rate, according to a Perth demolition contractor, risking electrocution and asbestos exposure to sell the scrap metal on the black market.

image.altText: Copper wiring bundles into circles

items.title: Owning a dog was nothing like I'd imagined

children.content: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.

gallery.altText: Bruce smiles as he holds his cattle cross dog Steady.
A dog of kelpie appearance stands on a balcony beside a wooden chair.
Steady the dog walks along a beach, with a big stick in the foreground.

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything

children.content: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

image.altText: A couples legs lying in bed

items.title: ICAC to probe allegations against University of Wollongong executives

children.content: The anti-corruption commission is to examine allegations about recruitment and the employment of a consultancy firm against Chancellor Michael Still and other members of the university's executive.

image.altText: Man sitting speaking into a microphone

items.title: Media mogul Antony Catalano has bail varied to allow travel to Byron Bay

children.content: Former ACM executive Antony Catalano will be allowed to visit his wife and kids after successfully applying to have his bail conditions changed.

image.altText: A smiling businessman

items.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed, report finds

children.content: Almost one-third of children in out-of-home care in the Northern Territory were allegedly harmed in the last financial year, according to a new report from the Office of the Children's Commissioner.

image.altText: Children's toys, shoes, coloring pens.

items.title: 'A life filled with honour': WA's longest-serving MP farewelled at state funeral

children.content: Hundreds of mourners farewell WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, at his state funeral in Perth.

gallery.altText: A portrait of Bill Grayden on a table during a state funeral.
Kokoda campaign veteran Bill Grayden at a service in Kings Park.
Pallbearers in military outfits carry a casket draped in an Australian flag up a cathedral aisle, with mourners either side.
